title: Vivid Dense Japanese Duck Gyoza
description: A sour and dense Japanese dish featuring wholegrain mustard and tempering chocolate, perfect for a date night, and sugar-free friendly.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the chopped duck breast, wholegrain mustard, tempering chocolate, green onions, soy sauce, sesame oil, and ginger, and mix well.
Lay a wonton wrapper on a flat surface and place a small spoonful of the duck mixture in the center of the wrapper.
Dip your finger in a small amount of water and run it along the edges of the wrapper.
Fold the wrapper into a triangle by bringing the two opposite corners together to form a point.
Press the edges together to seal the gyoza.
Repeat with the remaining wrappers and filling.
Heat a large skillet with a small amount of sesame oil over medium-high heat.
When the oil is hot, add the gyoza to the skillet and cook until the bottoms are golden brown.
Add a small amount of water to the skillet and cover with a lid to steam the gyoza.
Cook for an additional 5 minutes, then serve hot.
